title: Add operator-facing dropdown menu-bar item for Atelier + required Argus menu-leaf expansion
origin: MT-040 audit P4 (out of MT-006 scope) + operator goal directive 'make sure there is an operator facing drop down menu bar item for this wp'.

scope.summary: Add an operator-facing dropdown menu-bar entry for Atelier to the shell menu bar (handshake_menu_bar, app.rs ~7073-7075 / top_menu_bar.rs). The entry opens the Atelier full-window module and offers jump-to CKC / PoseKit / Ingest sub-module tabs. Because menu leaves are dynamically created only while a menu is open, this MT MUST also perform the mandated Argus expansion so a model can discover and steer menu leaves.

menu_bar_facts.menu_bar_panel: handshake_menu_bar (app.rs ~7073-7075); top-level menu buttons menu-file/-edit/-view/-go/-run/-help are Role::MenuItem with Action::Click/Focus + author_id (top_menu_bar.rs:149-156, :411-417); already Argus-visible (proven test_top_menu_bar.rs live_shell_has_six_top_level_menus_with_stable_ids :59).
menu_bar_facts.leaf_gap: Menu leaves (menu.file.quit, menu.edit.undo, menu.view.reset-layout, menu.go.command-palette, menu.run.swarm-board, menu.help.about, menu-go-* nav leaves) exist in the AccessKit tree ONLY while their parent menu is open (top_menu_bar.rs:78-82; asserted absent when closed test_top_menu_bar.rs:114-118). So Argus cannot one-shot a leaf.

acceptance_criteria: AC1: An operator-facing dropdown menu-bar entry exists for Atelier (either a new 'Atelier' top-level menu or an Atelier item under an existing menu) that opens the Atelier module and can select the CKC / PoseKit / Ingest sub-module tabs. Stable author_ids on the entry and its leaves.

AC2 (Argus expansion, MANDATORY per operator): implement a static leaf-id discovery registry (enumerate menu.<x>.<leaf> author_ids per menu) so Argus/models know valid leaf targets WITHOUT first opening the menu; OR expose the same commands via the command registry/palette as a flat, always-present addressable surface.

AC3 (Argus expansion): encode a two-step open-then-steer protocol (argus.click menu-<x> -> drain+repaint -> argus.inspect -> argus.click menu.<x>.<leaf>) and prove with a live test that argus.click on the menu OPENS and HOLDS the menu across the headless action-drain frame so the leaf is clickable (egui popups can close on the next synthesized pointer event).

AC4: Argus can inspect + steer the new Atelier menu entry and its leaves headlessly (HBR-VIS/QUIET). If Argus cannot see/steer, expand Argus (author_ids, AccessKit roles, inspection hooks) in this MT.
AC5: Update the in-product internal UserManual for the new menu entry + the Argus menu-leaf discovery/steer protocol (HBR-MAN).
AC6: No scaffolding; runtime proof (Spec-Realism Gate).

hbr_obligations_note: HBR-VIS (Argus menu inspect/steer), HBR-QUIET (headless, no focus/input steal), HBR-MAN (UserManual), HBR-SWARM (parallel-agent addressability of menu + leaves).
